etymology.description: 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") + 㡭(㡭) (jì, phonetic) = continuing threads -> to continue
etymology.interpretation: The character 繼(jì, "to continue") combines the semantic component 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread"), signifying threads or continuity, with a phonetic component 㡭(㡭) (jì), which together convey the meaning of continuing or passing on, similar to the unbroken nature of a thread.

examples.chinese: 请继续努力(請繼續努力)。
她继续讲故事(她繼續講故事)，孩子们听得入迷(孩子們聽得入迷)。
examples.pinyin: Tā jìxù jiǎng gùshì, háizimen tīng dé rùmí.
examples.english: She continued telling the story, and the children listened intently.

examples.chinese: 在会议上，大家决定继续这个计划(在會議上，大家決定繼續這個計畫)。
examples.pinyin: Zài huìyì shàng, dàjiā juédìng jìxù zhège jìhuà.
examples.english: At the meeting, everyone decided to continue with this plan.
